divergence: Every mirror shows next Tuesday, and fashion has never recovered
story_angle: solemn obituary for a beloved institution nobody can remember the purpose of
headline: Order of the Tuesday Hem-Scryers Disbands, Leaving Only Unfashionable Sleeves in Their Wake
deck: Hem-Scryers to blame for lack of sartorial splendor in the days to come — all because one petty decree declared their gazing unlawful.
article:
Archdeacon Qalireen the Eloquent signed the gazing ban with a flourish that ended the Tuesday Hem-Scryers overnight. Members could survive only by pinning on sigil tags whose meaning no one recalls.

Grandmaster Aiouea vanished into the Cobalt Drift. The bone-white spires hummed the same seven-note dirge for seven nights, a sound that now doubles as warning for the sleeves arriving next Tuesday.

Mirrors still show the coming garments. Citizens who once sought prophecy now see only the same dull cuffs and turn away.

Guaei, lately of the Order, summed it up: "We traded prophecy for fabric and the fabric is winning."
author: Eldric the Perpetually Pensive, Chronicler of the Cobalt Drift

weather.condition: Dismal sleeves expected, fashion crisis persists.

oped.title: The Scryers’ Exit: Finally, Sleeves We Can Trust
oped.author: Baron Crispin Velvet-Ruff, Chief Censor of Prophetic Linings
oped.body: At last the Tuesday Hem-Scryers have been silenced, and not a moment too soon. Their unlawful peering into next week’s hems was never about splendor; it was naked sabotage aimed squarely at my own seasonal collection of sensible wool cuffs. By declaring such gazing illegal we have restored order, even if every mirror now mocks us with last week’s stains. The victims complain of unfashionable sleeves, yet they forget that true nobility requires no foresight, only starch. Should any former scryer attempt to resume their trade, I propose we replace mirrors entirely with polished shields—my shields, of course, bearing my crest. Fashion may suffer, but at least no one will again question the length of a baron’s cuff. Progress demands sacrifice, preferably someone else’s hem.

classifieds.text: Pristine Tuesday mirrors, once blessed by the Hem-Scryers, now show only endless unfashionable sleeves. Must sell before the decree's shame reaches my stall.
Desperate tailor seeks sturdy lads to stitch new sleeves that might withstand next Tuesday's gaze. No scrying experience needed; pay in turnips.
Lost: one set of lawful hem-scrying gloves, left behind when the Order disbanded. Finder may keep the petty decree's guilt.
To the petty lord who banned our gazing: my sleeves now mock me daily. I confess and beg forgiveness before next Tuesday arrives.

panels.caption: The final Hem-Scryer hurls his mirror: 'Tuesday's sleeves are nothing but ragged flaps!'
The chamberlain stamps the scroll: 'Gazing at unlawful hems ends today—let the cuffs flop unseen.'
A knight tugs his stubby sleeve and mutters, 'At least next Tuesday won't mock us to our faces.'

joke.setup: When the Tuesday Hem-Scryers disbanded, the nobles demanded new sleeves to greet the week ahead.
joke.punchline: Their tailors replied that all cloth had already rotted in sympathy with last week's failed prophecies.
joke.text: When the Tuesday Hem-Scryers disbanded, the nobles demanded new sleeves to greet the week ahead. Their tailors replied that all cloth had already rotted in sympathy with last week's failed prophecies.

sponsor_ads.headline: Post-Scryer Sleeve Menders
sponsor_ads.body: With the Order disbanded and naught but ill sleeves remaining, our blind weavers stitch hems ignorant of Tuesday's coming mockery.
sponsor_ads.tagline: No glass consulted; satisfaction guaranteed until next week.

sponsor_ads.headline: Veiled Looking-Glasses
sponsor_ads.body: Cover thy mirror with our leaded cloth lest it betray the unfashionable sleeves the Order hath abandoned to all.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Suitable for any day save the one thou hast not yet reached.

sponsor_ads.headline: Tuesday Torch & Hem Service
sponsor_ads.body: Burn yesterday's sleeves by controlled flame to erase the mirror's vision of next week's disgrace now the scryers are gone.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Royal warrant forbids viewing on the actual Tuesday.

sponsor_ads.headline: Public Notice: Sleeve Surrender
sponsor_ads.body: All garments still showing the Order's unfashionable hems must be delivered to the square before the mirrors expose their shame to the realm.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Fines doubled for those who wait until Wednesday.

stories.headline: Tuesday Gaze Ban Forces Guildless Gasbags Into Sleeve-Less Drift
stories.deck: Consensus spires now sing only of exposed midriffs until 442.
stories.article:
Without certified forecasts, drifting citizens now judge next week by collar height alone. High collars dominate the promenades; hems remain a mystery.

Fabric merchants count unsold bolts and blame the lack of scryers rather than their own stock.

stories.headline: Consensus Harvests Delayed as Spire Choirs Refuse Tuesday Chants
stories.deck: Root crops on Kreion now ripen only after three days of eye-ring silence.
stories.article: Outer Drift farms wait for spire-song cues that no longer mention Tuesday. Vines swell without signal; clerks demand three extra witnesses before any root is tallied.

stories.headline: Mirror Calibration Guild Reports Universal Tuesday Offset of One Full Cycle
stories.deck: Next week's readings now arrive precisely three days prior to arrival.
stories.article: Every reflective surface now shows events one week early. The gazing ban blocks recalibration, so the constant Tuesday image slowly replaces all other data.

real_world_echo: Marine Le Pen headline: the Order was formally condemned for 'unlawful gazing' yet permitted to continue if every member wore a visible sigil tag, only to expire anyway when no one could recall why the sigils mattered
